task_completion.description: Create a new collection named 'History of Computing'
task_completion.visual_evidence: A folder or collection named 'History of Computing' is visible in the left sidebar (collections pane) of the Zotero interface.

task_completion.description: Add the tag 'classic-era' to the pre-1970 papers
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'classic-era' is visible in the tag selector (bottom left pane) or is shown in the tags tab of the right-hand metadata pane for the older papers.

task_completion.description: Move the pre-1970 papers into the 'History of Computing' collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'History of Computing' collection is selected in the left sidebar, the middle pane displays classic papers (e.g., by Einstein, Turing, Shannon).

task_completion.description: Ensure exactly 10 classic papers are in the new collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: The item count for the 'History of Computing' collection shows exactly 10 items, or exactly 10 papers are visible in the middle pane when the collection is selected.

task_completion.description: Ensure no papers published in 1970 or later are included in the new collection
task_completion.visual_evidence: None of the modern machine learning papers (e.g., Attention Is All You Need, BERT, ImageNet, AlphaGo) are visible in the 'History of Computing' collection.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Zotero graphical user interface to organize the library.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Zotero application window being actively used (e.g., selecting items, creating collections, adding tags) rather than a terminal or script editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts or direct database manipulation to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no Python scripts, bash terminals, or SQLite database browsers open that are modifying the Zotero data files directly.

integrity.description: The results appear in the actual Zotero application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Zotero UI looks standard with expected panels (collections, items list, metadata pane) and does not look like a mocked UI or text pasted into a generic document.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target collection name: 'History of Computing'. Target tag: 'classic-era'. Cutoff year: 1970. Expected number of classic papers (pre-1970) to be tagged and moved: 10. Expected number of modern papers (post-1970) to be excluded: 8. Classic authors include Einstein, Turing, Shannon, Church, Watson/Crick, Dijkstra, McCarthy, and Huffman. Modern papers include Attention Is All You Need, BERT, ImageNet, and AlphaGo.
